data.title: As a DSO agent running brainstorm or preplanning, I can query dark pattern taxonomies, cognitive load checklists, and design principle catalogs to identify harmful patterns and improve design quality
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Populates plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/patterns/ with dark pattern taxonomies, cognitive load patterns, and design principle catalogs: Brignull dark pattern taxonomy, SageSELab AidUI 27-class taxonomy, cognitive load and defensive design checklists, and representative entries from benbrignell/principles.design. Manually updates _index.yaml.

**Why**: This Enhancement story extends the principles layer (Story-5a) with specific pattern taxonomies that enable agents to identify harmful design patterns (dark patterns, cognitive overload) and reference community-maintained design principle collections.

**Scope**:
- IN: patterns/ directory — Brignull dark pattern taxonomy (12+ patterns), AidUI 27-class taxonomy, cognitive load + defensive design checklists, representative entries from benbrignell/principles.design; Summary + Detail required; license per source
- OUT: Core heuristics and principles (covered in Story-5a); WCAG/USWDS (Stories 3-4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, patterns/ directory covers Brignull dark pattern taxonomy (12+ named patterns), AidUI 27-class taxonomy, cognitive load and defensive design checklists, and representative entries from principles.design; each entry has YAML frontmatter with domain: [patterns] and Summary + Detail tiers; no entry file exceeds 500 lines
  ← Satisfies: SC4 (Brignull dark pattern taxonomy, AidUI, cognitive load, defensive design, principles.design)

- When this story is complete, _index.yaml is manually updated with all patterns/ entries and committed; check-corpus-schema.sh passes on all patterns/ files
  ← Satisfies: SC1, SC2

- When this story is complete, dso ref-query "dark patterns" returns at least one entry from Brignull taxonomy with domain: [patterns]
  ← Satisfies: SC4 + SC3

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] benbrignell/principles.design is MIT licensed — use representative entries, not the full catalog, to keep the patterns/ directory manageable; record selected entries in docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml
- [Reliability] AidUI (SageSELab/AidUI) is MIT licensed; Brignull dark patterns are published on deceptive.design — verify license and use paraphrase with attribution if verbatim copying is not permitted

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
